product.summary: All mandatory requirement blockers are fully populated and confirmed. The project defines a full production multi-model AI chat platform using Next.js, FastAPI, and Supabase, hosted across Vercel, Render, and Supabase. Security, privacy, and compliance baselines are advanced and well-articulated. The scope explicitly excludes an MVP phase, targeting a complete initial release.
blindspots: No explicit mention of scalability targets or concurrency expectations.
No defined approach for rate limiting or abuse prevention.
No explicit UX accessibility or localization requirements.
functional_gaps: Missing specification for model API key management and rotation.
Unclear how file uploads are processed, stored, and scanned for security.
No explicit admin or moderation interface requirements.
ux_considerations: Unified chat interface should clearly differentiate responses from different AI models.
Chat history and model comparison views need intuitive navigation and visual clarity.
Responsive design and accessibility compliance (WCAG 2.1 AA) should be ensured.
future_phase_candidates: Add user-configurable model orchestration or ensemble mode.
Introduce plugin or extension system for third-party integrations.
Implement advanced analytics dashboard for user engagement and model performance.
product.next_focus: Proceed to architectural planning phase, emphasizing secure LLM integration, data flow design, and operational scalability.

architect_reasoner.summary: The project will deliver a full-scale multi-model AI chat web application enabling users to interact with multiple AI models (ChatGPT, Gemini, Claude, etc.) through a unified interface. It targets the general public, with account-based access, persistent chat history, and advanced security and privacy baselines. The stack includes Next.js for the frontend, FastAPI for the backend, and Supabase for data management, hosted across Vercel, Render, and Supabase. The release is full production—no MVP phase.
architect_reasoner.feasibility: High. The chosen stack is modern, compatible, and well-supported. The architecture can scale horizontally with proper API gateway and caching layers. Security and compliance goals are achievable with standard frameworks and managed services. Resource constraints typical of a final-year project are manageable with modular design and cloud-managed components.
architect_reasoner.proposed_architecture_direction: Adopt a modular, service-oriented architecture with a secure backend API layer mediating all LLM interactions. The frontend (Next.js) communicates with FastAPI via REST or GraphQL endpoints. Supabase handles authentication, chat history, and file metadata. LLM adapters abstract each model provider (OpenAI, Anthropic, Google, etc.) behind a unified interface. All secrets and API keys are stored securely in backend environment variables or Supabase Vault. The system enforces zero-trust principles, encrypts chat data, and logs all access for auditability.

frontend: User authentication and session management (Supabase Auth integration)
Chat interface with multi-model tabs and comparison view
backend: Chat orchestration service (model routing, context management)
LLM adapter layer (OpenAI, Anthropic, Google, etc.)
File upload and scanning service (virus/malware check)
Audit logging and anomaly detection service
data: System configuration and API key registry

data_and_api_notes.data_flow: Frontend sends chat requests to FastAPI, which routes them through the LLM adapter layer. Responses are stored in Supabase for history retrieval. File uploads are scanned and stored securely, with metadata linked to user sessions.
data_and_api_notes.api_design: RESTful endpoints with JWT-based authentication. Rate limiting and abuse prevention middleware required. WebSocket or SSE channel for real-time chat streaming.
data_and_api_notes.security: All data in transit via HTTPS/TLS 1.3. Chat content encrypted at rest. API keys rotated periodically. Strict CORS and CSRF protection enforced.

infrastructure_direction.frontend: Deploy Next.js on Vercel with environment variables managed via Vercel Secrets.
infrastructure_direction.backend: Deploy FastAPI on Render with autoscaling enabled and background worker support for long-running LLM calls.
infrastructure_direction.database: Supabase for PostgreSQL storage, authentication, and file storage. Enable row-level security and encryption at rest.
infrastructure_direction.networking: Use HTTPS across all services, with API gateway or reverse proxy for unified domain routing.
infrastructure_direction.scalability: Horizontal scaling for backend workers and caching layer (e.g., Redis) for session and rate-limit management.

devops_direction.ci_cd: GitHub Actions for automated testing, linting, and deployment to Vercel/Render.
devops_direction.monitoring: Integrate Sentry for error tracking, Prometheus-compatible metrics for backend, and Supabase logs for database events.
devops_direction.observability: Centralized dashboard aggregating logs, metrics, and traces. Alerting for latency spikes, failed requests, and security anomalies.
devops_direction.backup_and_recovery: Daily database backups via Supabase. Versioned file storage. Disaster recovery plan with restore testing.

design_principles: Separation of concerns between frontend, backend, and data layers
Compliance alignment with GDPR and SOC2
Observability and auditability baked into all layers

security.summary: The project is a full-scale multi-model AI chat platform using Next.js, FastAPI, and Supabase, hosted on Vercel, Render, and Supabase respectively. It enables users to interact with multiple AI models through a unified interface, with account-based access, persistent chat history, and advanced security and privacy baselines. The architecture emphasizes modularity, secure backend-managed LLM integration, and compliance with privacy-by-design principles.
key_risks: Potential API rate-limit or cost overruns due to multiple LLM integrations.
Security exposure through file uploads if scanning or validation is insufficient.
Scalability challenges under high concurrent usage without caching or load balancing.
Compliance drift if retention and deletion policies are not strictly enforced.
User data leakage risk if encryption or access controls are misconfigured.
required_controls: Implement strict rate limiting and abuse prevention middleware.
Integrate antivirus and content scanning for all file uploads.
Use encrypted storage for all chat and user data (AES-256 at rest, TLS 1.3 in transit).
Rotate API keys and secrets periodically using secure vault management.
Enforce role-based access control (RBAC) and audit logging for all administrative actions.
Apply row-level security in Supabase and validate JWT tokens for every request.
privacy_notes: Chat data retained for 30 days, then automatically purged.
PII encrypted and deleted upon account closure.
Anonymous usage logs retained only for analytics, with no user identifiers.
User consent required for data retention and analytics tracking.
Support for user-initiated data deletion and export under GDPR.
compliance_notes: Align with GDPR and SOC2 principles: data minimization, encryption, and auditability.
Maintain documented data flow and retention policies.
Ensure third-party LLM providers comply with equivalent privacy and security standards.
Conduct periodic compliance audits and maintain evidence of encryption and deletion enforcement.
moderation_notes: Implement content filtering and moderation for user prompts and AI responses.
Provide admin dashboard for reviewing flagged content and abuse reports.
Use automated detection for harmful or disallowed content (e.g., hate speech, PII leakage).
Allow users to report problematic outputs directly from the chat interface.
incident_response_notes: Establish a 24-hour response SLA for security or data incidents.
Maintain audit logs and anomaly detection alerts for suspicious activity.
Define escalation paths for data breaches and service outages.
Perform quarterly incident response drills and maintain recovery documentation.
Ensure backup restoration testing and versioned rollback capability.

constraints.summary: The project will deliver a secure, full-scale multi-model AI chat web application using Next.js (frontend), FastAPI (backend), and Supabase (data platform). It enables users to interact with multiple AI models (ChatGPT, Gemini, Claude, etc.) through a unified interface, with account-based access, persistent chat history, and advanced security and privacy baselines. Hosting is distributed across Vercel, Render, and Supabase. The architecture emphasizes modularity, compliance, and maintainability, launching directly as a full production release without an MVP phase.
constraints.cost_range: USD $8,000–$15,000 (cloud hosting, API usage, development, and security tooling for a full production release under limited resource conditions).

complexity_profile.frontend: Medium–High (multi-model chat interface, real-time streaming, accessibility compliance).
complexity_profile.backend: High (LLM adapter orchestration, secure API routing, rate limiting, file scanning, audit logging).
complexity_profile.data: Medium (Supabase schema design, encryption, retention enforcement, row-level security).
complexity_profile.security: High (zero-trust, encryption, compliance, moderation, anomaly detection).
complexity_profile.devops: Medium (CI/CD, monitoring, backup, recovery, observability dashboard).

constraints.maintainability_notes: Modular architecture with clear separation of concerns between frontend, backend, and data layers. Use typed interfaces, automated tests, and documentation for each module. Employ environment-based configuration management and versioned API adapters for future model integrations. Supabase-managed authentication and storage reduce maintenance overhead. Regular dependency updates and CI/CD automation ensure long-term stability.

phased_delivery.phase_1: Core platform setup — Next.js frontend, FastAPI backend, Supabase integration, authentication, and chat orchestration.
phased_delivery.phase_2: Multi-model integration — implement adapter layer for GPT, Gemini, Claude, etc., with unified response handling and comparison view.
phased_delivery.phase_3: Security and compliance hardening — encryption, audit logging, rate limiting, file scanning, and privacy enforcement.
phased_delivery.phase_4: Observability and admin tools — monitoring dashboard, moderation interface, anomaly detection, and backup validation.
phased_delivery.phase_5: Final production deployment — load testing, accessibility audit, compliance verification, and public release.

tradeoffs.security_vs_performance: Strong encryption and audit logging may increase latency; caching and async processing mitigate impact.
tradeoffs.feature_completeness_vs_resource_limit: Full production scope under limited resources requires prioritizing core stability over advanced UX polish.
tradeoffs.multi-model_integration_vs_cost_control: Supporting multiple LLM providers increases API costs; dynamic rate limiting and usage quotas are essential.
tradeoffs.cloud_hosting_vs_self_management: Managed services (Vercel, Render, Supabase) simplify operations but limit low-level control and custom optimization.

implementation_pressure_points: LLM adapter layer complexity and API key rotation management.
File upload security (virus scanning, content validation).
Rate limiting and abuse prevention under concurrent usage.
Cross-service authentication consistency between Supabase and FastAPI.
Compliance enforcement for data retention and deletion policies.
Real-time chat streaming and frontend performance optimization.

critic.summary: All mandatory requirement blockers are confirmed and complete. The project is ready to advance to architectural planning. The specification defines a full production multi-model AI chat platform using Next.js, FastAPI, and Supabase, hosted on Vercel, Render, and Supabase respectively. Security, privacy, and compliance baselines are advanced and well-defined. The architecture direction, security controls, and phased delivery plan are coherent and feasible under the stated constraints.
contradictions: The project is described as a 'final-year-project' with limited resources but simultaneously targets a full production-grade release without an MVP phase, which may conflict with resource constraints.
blindspots: No explicit scalability or concurrency targets defined.
No detailed rate limiting or abuse prevention strategy beyond general mention.
No explicit accessibility or localization requirements.
No defined UX testing or usability validation plan.

unresolved_questions.Q1: What are the expected peak concurrent users or throughput targets to guide scalability planning?
unresolved_questions.Q2: Should the platform support localization or multilingual UI for global users?
unresolved_questions.Q3: What is the acceptable latency threshold for chat response streaming?
unresolved_questions.Q4: Are there budget or quota limits for third-party LLM API usage?

corrective_actions: Clarify scalability and concurrency expectations to size infrastructure and caching layers appropriately.
Define rate limiting, quota management, and abuse prevention mechanisms in measurable terms.
Add accessibility and localization requirements to ensure inclusivity and compliance with WCAG 2.1 AA.
Specify performance and latency targets for chat streaming and model response handling.
Reconcile the 'final-year-project' constraint with the 'full production release' goal by defining minimum viable production readiness criteria.
priority_order: Define rate limiting and abuse prevention strategy.
Reconcile resource constraints with full production scope.
Define LLM API usage budget and quota policies (Q4).
